featured_analysis.title: The Recurrent Obligation Problem
featured_analysis.description: Why displacement policy fails when recurrent costs remain outside ordinary budget systems.

featured_analysis.title: Institutional Absorption Capacity
featured_analysis.description: A framework for assessing how ordinary systems absorb displacement-related vulnerability over time.

featured_analysis.title: Pricing Displacement Risk
featured_analysis.description: How development banks, sovereign lenders, and ESG frameworks could treat displacement as a material governance risk.

featured_analysis.title: Southeast Asian Governance Gaps
featured_analysis.description: A comparative view of law, social protection, fiscal architecture, and regional engagement across eight states.

publications.title: Governing Displacement: Institutional Fault Lines and Sustainable Inclusion
publications.abstract: Analysis of how institutional arrangements shape the transition from short-term response to sustainable inclusion.

publications.title: Development-Induced Displacement and State Absorption Capacity
publications.abstract: Explores how state systems absorb displacement pressures generated by development, infrastructure, and mobility transitions.

publications.title: Social Protection and Displacement
publications.abstract: Examines the role of national protection systems in supporting displaced populations and host communities.

publications.title: Fiscal Architecture and the Recurrent Obligation Problem
publications.abstract: Considers how public finance systems can account for the sustained costs of displacement inclusion.

notes.title: Why the Monitor marks judgement-based cells
notes.summary: A working dataset can be useful without pretending to be final. Judgement markers show where evidence still needs triangulation.

notes.title: Displacement as a budget problem
notes.summary: The policy question is not only whether assistance exists, but whether costs are carried by recurrent public systems.

notes.title: Regional governance and ordinary systems
notes.summary: Regional frameworks matter most when they shape how national systems include people in practice.

notes.title: Development finance exposure
notes.summary: Infrastructure and sovereign finance can produce or absorb displacement risk, but the risk is rarely priced explicitly.

research_in_progress: Development banks and institutional absorption capacity
